sessions.lever: session 1 — HEAD form (committed score 20) with int new_var/2 padding + if (!D_8009BE7A){} empty-if scaffold
session 2 — clean v8 form (unconditional var_a1 = arg1 preload + strip padding)

workflow round 1: 7 fresh structural variants on top of the score-12 candidate baseline (inverted-goto-merge X-clamp, X mask precompute mirror of Y SOTN duplicate-read, s16 xlim explicit block-local, var_v0_2 = arg0 initial-then-overwrite, s16 ylim block-local, u16 var_v0_2 narrowing, OR-chain associativity reorderings) + move of var_a1=arg1 preload to after the X clamp
sessions.note: Confirmed documented score-12 baseline reproduces. Disassembly diff vs target confirms WIP's structural-ceiling claim exactly: idx 0 `move a2,a0` (mine) vs `move a3,a0` (target) — X-preserve register, cc1 ascending-allocator picks the lower-numbered $a2 — then cascades through ~12 register-rotation diffs including the missing target idx 16 `move a3,v0` park-merge. No tested structural perturbation flips this without invoking forbidden constructs (literal renames, UB-conditional-init, dead-conditional store). Corroborates session 3's ~5400-iter permuter finding that only UB-conditional-init forms close further. Honest pure-C floor = 12.

sessions.lever: workflow round 2: BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG (tmp/gccdbg/cc1) instrumented cc1 dump on score-12 candidate AND sibling func_8007C748 (matched 3-arg) AND func_8007C938 (matched 2-arg dispatch sibling) + 13 fresh structural variants
sessions.note: ALLOCDBG MECHANISM EVIDENCE: at score-12 form, cc1 emits 11 pseudos. The X-preserve pseudo is #72 (nrefs=2 livelen=11 pri=1818, ord=10 LAST) and gets $a2 (reg 6). Target wants it in $a3 (reg 7). KEY FINDING: variant v6 (`s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78` block-local) IS THE FIRST KNOWN LEVER that flips the X-preserve to $a3 — ALLOCDBG confirms a new pseudo 91 (nrefs=3 livelen=7 pri=4285) gets $a2 at ord=6, and pseudo 76 (X-preserve role, ord=8) flips to $a3. v6 also produces target's missing idx-16 `move a3,v0` park-merge. BUT v6 scores 18 (REGRESSION) because lim_x's lifetime forces an extra early `move $3,$4` preserve and shifts pseudo 77 from $a2 to $a1, netting +6 cascade diffs. Three additional ALLOCDBG takeaways: (1) C748 has only 5 pseudos, args take $a0/$a1/$a2 naturally — fundamentally different shape; (2) C938 has only 4 pseudos and uses `new_var2 = arg0` as SOTN-allowed named-intermediate (works because no X-clamp clobbers $a0); (3) X-preserve in C86C is structurally necessary (target's idx-12-14 reuses $a0 for `lim_x-1` then re-reads preserve in branch delay slot at idx 14 `addu v0, a3, $zero`). Mechanism for flipping the X-preserve to $a3 NOW CHARACTERIZED: introduce pseudo with priority > pseudo-72 but lifetime/refs that DON'T also bias pseudo 76/77's allocation. v6 is the existence proof. The score-12 candidate remains the practical floor under explored named-intermediate / decl-order / operand-flip lever class. v17 (v6 + no widemask precompute) scored 23 = worst regression.

sessions.lever: workflow round 3: 12 micro-variant sweep on lim_x form per round-2 v6 hypothesis (i/ii/iii/iv/v/vi categories + structural variants v18-v29)
sessions.note: All 12 variants either fold back to candidate's score-12 form (combine-foldable shapes — v23 s16 lim_x at 12) or match v6's score-18 form (cost-equivalent shapes with the same lifetime-induced cascade — v19, v20, v21, v22, v26, v28 all at 18) or regress further (v18 at 14, v24 at 21, v25 at 22, v27 at 20, v29 at 22). CONCLUSION: every variant that introduces a separate pseudo for lim_x (s32-width, livelen > 0) emits the v6 cost (+6 cascade). Every variant that folds back to no-separate-pseudo (s16 lim_x, lim_x referenced once before combine) matches candidate's score 12. There is no middle ground in the explored shape space. This is the same v0 + +1insn-trap that defeats cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec siblings — documented 'lever-class structural ceiling' per register-alloc-pure-c sessions 7-10. ENVIRONMENT NOTE: `--keep-cheat-asm` flag is the workaround for the sandbox truncation error blocking round-2 C97C/CBB0 workers — the `cheat_asm_stripped: 383` blocks throughout display.c break sibling reorder rules; keeping cheat-asm (since the function under test has none in the body) preserves indices and yields the masked-distance score normally.

reviewer.evidence: Not yet invoked on the score-12 form. Worker self-audit: PASS (same 4 lever stages as C7A0, each with semantic purpose — the SOTN duplicate-read is the resolved-ALLOWED technique). Independent cheat-reviewer verdict still owed before any Match: commit.

remaining_gap.summary: Identical to func_8007C7A0: $a2-vs-$a3 X-preserve register tiebreaker cascade. Missing park insn at idx 16 + X-preserve register choice ($a2 mine vs $a3 target) cascade through ~12 register-rotation diffs. The only difference vs C7A0 is the constant (0xE4000000 vs 0xE3000000).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 21 substs in regfix.txt:3073-3094 calibrated for HEAD form. Candidate's score-12 shape produces SHA1 947ff79e... (mismatch). Same lateral-movement reason as C7A0 — rules would need re-writing for the new shape with no net rule reduction; commit gated on lowering the score further (toward 0).

next_hypotheses: Workflow round 3 (2026-06-03) outcomes — PRIORITY 1: Lever D-style chain-extender is FORBIDDEN per 2026-06-02 rule on cpu_side_move_dir_4's combine-foldable chain-extender; if the chain-extender is still forbidden in updated rules, this avenue is closed. PRIORITY 2: try BB2_PRIO_DEBUG instrumented cc1 on candidate vs v6 (tmp/gccdbg/cc1 referenced in register-alloc-pure-c.md session 7) — would reveal which dependency edges create v6's extra `move $3, $4` preserve at idx 0, the target for elimination. PRIORITY 3: try a `register asm("$a3") = arg0` PIN on the candidate baseline (diagnostic only, never commit) to confirm whether even pinning the X-preserve to $a3 induces the cascade — if the pin holds at score 18 it confirms the cascade is structural; if it lands lower it indicates GCC has a path to the target alloc we haven't found via C structure. PRIORITY 4: escalate to user as a likely candidate for canonical-asm authorization OR for the global rodata reorder route shared with the cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec cluster.

Workflow round 2 (2026-06-02) v6 finding (`s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78` block-local) is the FIRST KNOWN LEVER that flips C86C's X-preserve pseudo to target's $a3 — but it costs +6 score points due to (a) early `move $3,$4` and (b) pseudo 77 cascade to $a1. Next session target: ELIMINATE those costs via (1) PERMUTER FROM v6 BASE — directed PERM_GENERAL on prologue region from the score-18 lim_x form, ~30k iters, vet output against cheat catalog (no UB-conditional-init, no dead-conditional-store, no param-aliases); OR (2) MICRO-VARIANT SWEEP ON LIM_X SHAPE — try (i) lim_x declared outer-scope but only read in if-branch, (ii) lim_x = `s16` (vs s32 — test if sign-extension penalty can be bypassed), (iii) lim_x = `(s32)D_8009BE78` explicit cast, (iv) lim_x = `D_8009BE78 - 1` directly, (v) lim_x read once for comparison only with `var_v0_2 = D_8009BE78 - 1` (replacing lim_x - 1 in the assignment). The mechanism dump shows pseudo 91 emerges in v6; the question is whether a different lim_x shape produces an EQUIVALENT pseudo 91 without the early preserve emit. ALSO: run BB2_PRIO_DEBUG on candidate vs v6 to verify which dependency edges create v6's extra preserve insn — points at the exact RTL-level chain to break in C source. JOINT WITH C7A0: iterate v6 finding on C7A0 too (constant 0xE3 vs 0xE4 is the only difference — should produce SAME ALLOCDBG flip).

Joint instrumented `BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG` cc1 dump of func_8007C86C at the score-12 form + sibling func_8007C748 (3-arg, matched pure-C) — same as the cpu_side_move_dir_4 session 5 lever discovery method. The dump exposes the priority gap between the pseudo getting $a2 (mine) vs the equivalent in C748 that achieves a natural higher-callee-save landing. If C748's allocation table shows a specific livelen/refs profile that flips the tiebreaker, search for a combine-foldable pure-C lever that mimics it in C86C without emitting bytes (analogous to C5MD4's tbl_125c+delta chain). Also worth checking: ALLOCDBG with the Y wide-mask precompute already applied (i.e. the score-12 form), since prior sessions only ran it on the score-17 clean v8 base.
tools/decomp-permuter from the score-12 base (NOT the score-17 base session 3 used). Directed at the X-preserve register slot. CRITICAL: invoke cheat-reviewer on output.
ALLOCDBG-instrumented dump on the score-12 form to identify cc1's $a2-vs-$a3 tiebreaker mechanism.
Sibling func_8007C748 (3-arg variant, matched pure-C) .greg cross-reference.
Joint lever-search with C7A0 — the two functions share the structural ceiling exactly; a lever found for one will apply to the other.

rejected_forms.form: (not tested directly here; same s16 raw_arg0 = arg0 literal-rename chain documented for C7A0)
rejected_forms.reason: Same FORBIDDEN form as memory/wip/func_8007C7A0/meta.json rejected_forms[0] — not re-tested this session because the verdict is the same.

rejected_forms.form: UB conditional-init patterns from session 3's permuter (~5400 iters)
rejected_forms.reason: Every closing form the directed permuter found used UB-conditional-init (a local read before any path writes it). Forbidden cheat-by-spelling per .claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md. See memory/project/func-8007c86c-permuter-ub-cheat-findings.md.

rejected_forms.form: Initial var_v0_2 = arg0 before the if/else clamp (early-init + conditional overwrite)
rejected_forms.reason: Regression AND is borderline cheat-by-spelling — the initial store is dead on two of three paths (arg0<0 overwrites with 0, arg0>limit-1 overwrites with limit-1) and only used on the fall-through path. Same shape family as the UB-conditional-init permuter rejected in session 3. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Inverted goto-merge for X clamp (`if arg0<0 {v=0; goto post_x;} if limit<arg0 {v=limit; goto post_x;} v=arg0; post_x:`)
rejected_forms.reason: Regression. Explicit goto-merge doesn't induce cc1 to emit a real park-merge insn; the merge label gets coalesced by jump-thread/CSE and the cascade gets WORSE (16 vs 12). (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: X-mask precompute (`var_v0 = var_v0_2 & 0xFFF;` before dispatch, narrow-mode overwrites)
rejected_forms.reason: Mirror of the Y wide-mask SOTN duplicate-read lever doesn't translate to X. The X-precompute happens between Y-precompute and the mode-dispatch and disturbs the bnez delay slot scheduling — narrow-arm needs the overwrite, wide-arm uses the precompute, but the combined emission is 2 insns worse than the bare candidate. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: s16 xlim block-local in arg0 clamp (`s16 xlim = D_8009BE78 - 1; if (xlim < arg0) ...`)
rejected_forms.reason: Same regression family as session 2's documented xlim test (which scored 25 from clean v8 base). Block-local adds an extra sign-extension insn for xlim that target doesn't have. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: s16 ylim block-local in arg1 clamp
rejected_forms.reason: Same regression family — extra sign-extension insn. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: OR-chain reordering `return var_v0 | 0xE4000000 | var_v1`
rejected_forms.reason: Slight regression (12 -> 13). cc1's combine canonicalises associativity differently from this source layout; the `(var_v1 | (var_v0 | 0xE4000000))` form (= r_e4 named-intermediate form) is the minimum. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v1 (round 2): operand-flip on X clamp comparison (`if (arg0 > (D_8009BE78 - 1))` per compare-operand-order-register.md)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — score 12 unchanged. The compare-operand-order lever works for sibling-call wrappers where LHS is a local and target's allocation flows from RTL-emission order, but here the operand swap is folded by combine before allocno priority kicks in. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v2 (round 2): operand-flip on BOTH X and Y comparisons
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — same combine fold as v1. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v3 (round 2): var_a1 = arg1 preload moved to AFTER X-clamp (instead of at function top)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — arg1's pseudo lifetime is bounded by its uses regardless of preload timing; cc1's pseudo creation order is RTL-emission order, not declaration order. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v4 (round 2): param mutation — write clamp result to arg0 itself (instead of var_v0_2)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION — does NOT flip X-preserve register to $a3 (ALLOCDBG confirms pseudo 72 still gets $a2). Borderline cheat-by-spelling (parameter mutation as result-holder) but irrelevant to the lever question. Differs from rejected_forms[2]'s var_v0_2 = arg0 initial-overwrite cheat. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v5 (round 2): s16 lim_x = D_8009BE78 block-local in X-clamp (read full halfword once, use twice as lim_x - 1)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — block-local lim_x folded back to inline form by combine because s16 forces sign-extension that combine eliminates. Distinct from prior xlim test (which scored 20 — held the -1 form). (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v6 (round 2): s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78 block-local (full halfword as s32 to avoid sign-extension) — THE FIRST KNOWN LEVER that flips X-preserve pseudo to $a3
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION but WITH MECHANISM EVIDENCE — IS the first known lever that flips X-preserve pseudo to target's $a3 (ALLOCDBG confirms pseudo 76 → reg 7 at ord=8 instead of candidate's pseudo 72 → reg 6 at ord=10). Also produces target's missing idx-16 `move a3, v0` park-merge. COST: lim_x's lifetime introduces extra early `move $3, $4` preserve and shifts pseudo 77 to $a1 (target $a2), netting +6 cascade diffs. THIS IS THE NEXT-SESSION BASELINE FOR DIRECTED PERMUTER. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v7 (round 2): s32 lim_x AND s32 lim_y block-local (both clamps)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. lim_y mirrors v6's effect on Y-clamp slot but compounds the cascade. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v8 (round 2): var_v0_2 s32 retype (vs s16) — eliminates one sign-extension
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. Changes upper-clamp result width which propagates through mask operations differently from target. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v9 (round 2): Y-clamp BEFORE X-clamp (change pseudo creation order)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. Early Y-clamp lengthens arg0's lifetime, exacerbating the preserve cascade. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v10 (round 2): declare var_v0_2 LAST (after var_v0, var_v1) — declaration order swap
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — confirms ALLOCDBG finding that pseudo numbers for autos follow RTL-emission order, not C declaration order. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v11 (round 2): no var_a1 preload — Y-clamp writes directly to arg1 parameter (in-place clamp)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — arg1 is read after writes (mask), so this is NOT the forbidden Lever D pattern. Same allocation as candidate. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v12 (round 2): OR-chain swap `return (var_v0 | 0xE4000000) | var_v1`
rejected_forms.reason: Minor regression. cc1 canonicalises OR associativity differently from this source layout. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v13 (round 2): drop precomputed `var_v1 = var_a1 & 0xFFF` and inline mask+shift in each dispatch arm
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. Removes the SOTN duplicate-read precompute lever proven necessary (session 4 evidence). (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v14 (round 2): no widemask precompute + var_a1 & 0xFFF inside narrow else only
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. Same as v13 effectively — drops the duplicate-read benefit. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v15 (round 2): decl swap (var_a1 before var_v0_2)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat — same as v10, decl order does not affect cc1 pseudo numbering. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v16 (round 2): s32 limit_minus_one = D_8009BE78 - 1 named intermediate (vs v6's lim_x which holds full value)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION — same effect family as v6 (the -1 form vs the full form behaves identically wrt allocno priority). (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v17 (round 2): v6 (lim_x) + no widemask precompute
rejected_forms.reason: WORST REGRESSION. Two simultaneous lever interventions cancel/compound badly. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v18 (round 3): lim_x compare-only + var_v0_2 = D_8009BE78 - 1 (re-read global) in assignment
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION 12 -> 14 with build_insns dropping to 49. Combine eliminates one of the duplicate reads but the resulting allocation is worse than candidate (lim_x's pseudo gets a brief lifetime, X-preserve picks wrong reg). (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v19 (round 3): s32 lim_x = (s32)D_8009BE78 (explicit cast variant of v6)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 18 — same as v6. Explicit (s32) cast doesn't change combine behavior on the lim_x load. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v20 (round 3): s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78 - 1 used twice (pre-subtract)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 18 — same as v6 family. Pre-subtracting the -1 into lim_x doesn't change priority calculation; cc1 emits the same allocation as v6. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v21 (round 3): s32 lim_x at function scope (outer decl), assigned inside if-branch only
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 18 — same as v6. cc1's pseudo creation order is RTL-emission order, not decl scope; outer decl with conditional assignment behaves identically to inner-scope decl. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v22 (round 3): v6 + var_a1 = arg1 deferred to inside both branches of the X-clamp
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 18 — same as v6. Deferring var_a1's preload position doesn't shift arg1's pseudo lifetime in a way that helps. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v23 (round 3): s16 lim_x = D_8009BE78 (narrow type variant of v6)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 12 — folds back to candidate's score. s16 forces sign-extension that combine eliminates, collapsing lim_x to inline form. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03, confirms rejected_forms v5 entry from round 2.)

rejected_forms.form: v24 (round 3): s32 lim_x AND s32 lim_y symmetric in both clamps
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION 18 -> 21. lim_y mirrors v6's effect on Y-clamp slot and compounds the cascade. Matches rejected_forms v7 from round 2. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v25 (round 3): s32 lim_x at function scope, UNCONDITIONALLY loaded at top (vs v21 conditional assignment)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION 18 -> 22. Unconditional load of D_8009BE78 outside the arg0>=0 branch forces lim_x's lifetime to extend across the entire function, severely disturbing allocation of arg1/var_a1 and the Y-clamp slot. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v26 (round 3): s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78 - 1, compared as `lim_x < arg0`, used as var_v0_2 = lim_x (no -1 needed since pre-subtracted)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 18 — same as v6 family. Equivalent to v20 but with single-use lim_x; folds to v6's allocation pattern. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v27 (round 3): flip branch direction — primary case `arg0 < lim_x` -> var_v0_2 = arg0, else lim_x - 1
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION 18 -> 20, build_insns drops to 48. The flipped comparison induces combine to drop a sign-compare, but the resulting branch layout doesn't match target. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v28 (round 3): s32 lim_x AND s32 lim_x_m1 = lim_x - 1 as separate locals (forced split)
rejected_forms.reason: Flat 18 — same as v6. The two-local form just emits the same combine-folded code as v6 (lim_x_m1 folds into use sites). (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: v29 (round 3): inverted if structure — `if (arg0 < 0) { var_v0_2 = 0; } else { lim_x ... }`
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION 18 -> 22. Inverting the outer if doesn't change the resulting decision-tree codegen (cc1 applies branch-sense canonicalization), but the source-order change disturbs scheduling. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

sibling_impact: func_8007C7A0 is the constant-differs sibling (0xE3 vs 0xE4). Both at floor 12 under the same lever stack. See memory/wip/func_8007C7A0/.
